query: Models complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Use This Simple Model to Resolve Common Customer Service Complaints
results.content:
There is, however, a fine line between efficiently routing calls and delivering a frustrating customer experience. If a customer complains about getting stuck in an endless electronic or even live help loop, your representatives should be prepared to listen, acknowledge, and resolve this very real problem. Representatives should be trained in understanding the phone systems so they can provide guidance on how customers can more efficiently get the help they need. This training may involve role-playing, in which one trainee assumes the part of the customer. Without a simple model in place, many service representatives apologize for the poor experience but move on too quickly from this complaint without acknowledging or taking the next steps toward a complete resolution. [...] Rather than simply mirroring a customer’s dissatisfaction with a prior representative’s lack of knowledge, put our model into place. Listen carefully to the customer’s tone and try to empathize with the frustration and the problems that a lack of knowledge may have caused the customer. In acknowledging the problem, explain how your business values employee training, and has a rigorous process. Let the customer know that several employees have been going through the instruction sequence and are close to reaching proficiency. [...] ### Example #1: Confusing automated telephone systems or endless transfers

Every business streamlines its phone support in one way or another. It’s a fact of life for customers and clients that getting to live help isn’t as easy as it once was. Today’s customer expectations reflect that reality.

results.title: Three Common Issues In Broken Attribution Models (And How To Overcome Them)
results.content:
As such, building an accurate attribution model that tracks this information for your industry can be difficult. Proper installation of key measurement technologies, like tracking pixels in every piece of your campaign, and statistically significant models that match the goals of your campaign, are vital. Otherwise, your insights can be far from accurate. A broken attribution model is worse than no information at all because it can send you in the entirely wrong direction.

There are three common problems with broken attribution models that you should watch for carefully:

1. Gaps In Attribution

The way people buy has evolved rapidly in recent years. Because of this, several major gaps remain in attribution models related to: [...] Multi-touch attribution modeling can be as straightforward or as complicated as you need it to be, but be realistic about what you can manage based on experience and budget. Your attribution model may be broken, but going from last-click attribution to a more complex multi-channel analysis for all channels can lead to more issues than it resolves.  
  
 By properly tagging your campaigns, understanding your attribution gaps and measuring external factors, you can ensure greater accuracy and insights from your model.

Editorial StandardsReprints & Permissions

LOADING VIDEO PLAYER...
FORBES’ FEATURED Video [...] How do you avoid making biased adjustments to your advertising, such as throttling your ad spend on a seasonal or competitive speculation when pricing has such a profound impact? When possible, setting a pricing control to measure the actual effectiveness of a given ad campaign is recommended. At the very least, consider the pricing and seasonality impact independent of your attribution metrics.

3. Multi-Touch Attribution

Multi-touch attribution (MTA) offers a more holistic look at the buyer’s journey, going beyond first or last touch, and can also create several problems that can break your attribution model. These include:

results.title: Five Problems Faced by Modeling Clients - TTS Blog
results.content:
## 5. Model Lacks Desired Functionality

Problem  
The model was built for a specific purpose and served that need well initially, but new functionality is needed as the client’s requirements evolve. The client may be proficient in Excel, but might lack the time or expertise to add these features without compromising the model. There is a fear of “breaking” the existing functionality. [...] ## 1. File is Too Slow or Large

Problem  
A large, slow model can be a productivity killer, with calculations taking multiple seconds—or even minutes—whenever changes are made. Models that have been expanded repeatedly over time tend to accumulate unused features, redundant formulas, and excessive calculations, making the file practically unusable. Clients often hesitate to delete any parts of the model, fearing it might be essential to other areas of the model operating correctly. [...] Problem  
A poorly organized model can be a nightmare to navigate. Models that attempt to “do it all” often end up disorganized, with overlapping inputs, complex calculations squeezed into outputs, and a general lack of clarity. Unclear models make it challenging to know where inputs can be adjusted or where calculations are happening, often requiring an advanced understanding of the model just to make small changes.

results.title: Customer complaints: Top 7 common complaints & how to resolve ...
results.content:
Staff confusion

When customers reach out for support, they become flustered if they reach a call center agent who can only provide scripted responses that don’t resolve their problem. They’re also frustrated if they’ve previously talked to another customer support agent, their new rep doesn’t have the context of the situation, and they need to start from scratch. Make sure that your customer support tools can gather the context of your customer’s problem in advance so you know exactly where to route him for help. [...] #### Complicated interactive voice response (IVR) system

Customers can quickly become frustrated if they can’t access the right department with the choices offered on the interactive voice response (IVR) system or have no option to talk to a live customer support agent. A survey conducted by CSG found that 36% of respondents would rather wait on hold to speak with a human agent than use an AI-powered virtual assistant to resolve their issue. [...] If you run a restaurant, you can generally tell who your unhappy customers are. They’ll be the ones scowling at their overcooked food or glaring at their phones while waiting too long for a dish. If your servers pay close attention, ask for feedback often, and work to make problems right, they should be able to turn negative experiences into positive ones. They can also avoid frustrated diners turning to Yelp to write one-star reviews or blasting your brand on social media with posts filled with customer complaints.

results.title: The Challenges Female Models Face and How to Overcome Them
results.content:
## How Does Modeling Affect Physical and Mental Health?

The demands of modeling can affect both body and mind. Constant travel, tight deadlines, and the need to stay camera-ready create pressure that builds up over time.

### What Health Issues Do Female Models Face?

Exhaustion from long days on set and irregular hours.

Unhealthy eating habits, especially when trying to meet sample size requirements.

Anxiety, depression, or isolation caused by the pressure to look a certain way or stay relevant.

### How Can Female Models Stay Healthy?

Prioritize sleep, balanced meals, and mental rest. These habits support long-term career success.

Speak with a counselor or therapist when needed. Mental health is just as important as physical health. [...] ## What Safety Risks Do Female Models Face?

Safety is a concern, especially when working alone or with unfamiliar professionals. Many female models enter the industry at a young age and may not be aware of their rights or the risks associated with it.

### What Are the Most Common Issues?

Unprofessional behavior, such as inappropriate comments or advances from photographers or clients.

Power imbalances where female models feel they must agree to uncomfortable requests to avoid losing work.

A lack of support systems. Most modeling jobs do not have human resources departments or clear complaint channels.

### How Can Female Models Stay Safe?

Research everyone involved in a job before accepting it. Look for reviews and examples of past work. [...] While some areas of modeling are more flexible today, high fashion still favors a narrow definition of beauty.

### How Does This Pressure Affect Female Models?

Strict expectations around appearance can impact mental and physical health. Female models may struggle with self-esteem, eating disorders, or the stress of maintaining a specific look.

The pressure can lead to burnout, especially when combined with rejection or public criticism. This also limits representation.

Female models who do not fit these standards may find fewer opportunities in traditional campaigns.

### What Can Female Models Do About It?

Work with agencies that support body diversity. John Casablancas International and similar agencies promote a more inclusive approach.
